name: Web Charts
description: Standard for the chart / data-visualization domain — a declarative, browser-aligned way to specify a chart's semantic intent (what the data means) kept strictly separate from its presentation (how it's drawn, consuming Web Theme tokens). The spec adopts a Vega-Lite L1 profile as the thin semantic core (L2+ tiers additive) and defines a CustomChartRenderer Protocol — a renderer-swap registry with a native-first SVG default, so adapters (Vega, Plotly, ECharts) plug in behind one contract. Conformance scores three axes: semantic fidelity, theme application, and accessibility (a first-class axis, not an afterthought). Ratified in #105 and scaffolded under epic #570.
tierEvidence: benchmarkCoverage covers the data-visualization domain; the named framework Vega-Lite is adopted as the semantic core (FUI ships charts/SvgChartRenderer.ts as the native-first renderer + Vega/Plotly/ECharts adapters).
